title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs events, held state, actions, remapping, focus, and devices from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for device-independent remappable action layer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.
Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 9443 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

Inspect the Input Accessibility trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Input Accessibility.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Input Accessibility, keep Input Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Input Accessibility case record

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 9443 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause. Artifact under review: device-independent remappable action layer.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. The record names the assumption, applies Input Accessibility, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Input Accessibility record says device-independent remappable action layer looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Input Accessibility.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Focus Device Loss · changed case · Review Focus Device Loss
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 6919 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Release latched actions and degrade safely when window focus, controller instance, battery, or audio device changes.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Event Queue has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Event Queue, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Event Queue precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Event Queue, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A review Asteroids team handles case 1157 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Event Queue, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Polling key state eliminates the need to process events.

options.text: Declare Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player outcome, revision, runtime, seed, input trace, accessibility profile, and frame, entity, collision, audio, and memory budgets for the review Event Queue case
Predict Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, failure, pause, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the review Event Queue case
Run the smallest deterministic Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python simulation model and retain exact intermediate and changed-case results for the review Event Queue case

Change one Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python frame rate, stall, seam, device, collision, cue, save, package, or release condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state desktop transfer limits for the review Event Queue case

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Input Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 3872 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Input Accessibility.
steps.why: Retrieving Input Accessibility through the concrete device-independent remappable action layer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Input Accessibility, keep Input Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Input Accessibility, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Adding controller support automatically makes input accessible.
